name: The Quiet Grandeur: Tales of Trees and Tombs
description: Sunder Nursery is easily one of the most soul-soothing spots in Delhi—it’s that perfect mix of Mughal grandeur and lush botany. Beyond the manicured lawns of Sunder Nursery lies a 16th-century saga waiting to be told. Join us for a heritage walk through the Azim Bagh, where we swap the city noise for tales of Mughal architecture, restored grandeur, and the secrets of the trees & monuments that stood the test of time.The walk is part of the March Tree Festival organised by SeasonWatch.

location.address: Bharat Scouts and Guides Marg, opposite Humayun Tomb Park, Nizamuddin, Hazrat Nizamuddin, National Zoological Park, Sundar Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi 110013, India

name: Overthinkers Anonymous
description: OverviewEver wondered why you default to people-pleasing, perfectionism, or avoidance? This session is about connecting the dots between your past experiences and your present reactions in a safe, judgment-free environment.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community-led initiative dedicated to creating safe, mindful spaces. Through workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities, from mental well-being to personal growth, with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectPattern Recognition: Explore why tendencies like overthinking or control keep showing up in your life.Guided Reflection: Use a specialized worksheet to gain context on your reactions without the weight of self-judgment.Awareness & Compassion: A gentle space to build self-awareness and understanding—focused on insight, not on reliving trauma.Details🗓️ Date: 22nd March (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Kanchanjunga Towers, Sector-56🎟️ Contribution: Rs 700 (Includes coffee + snacks)

location.address: A-BLOCK, KANCHANJUNGA APARTMENT, G.H.18, near Post Office, Sector 56, Gurugram, Haryana 122011, India

name: Growing a Garden
description: Growing a Garden - Nurturing an Ecosystem in the CityMuch can be grown in small spaces, in containers, or even as a hedge. You don't necessarily need a sprawling backyard, or lots of time.It's not difficult to harness nature in the city. What we need is a change in attitude and a spot in the sun. Join Anita, a professional Landscape Architect with experience in designing Edible Landscapes, and her Mum, Bimla, who has maintained a Kitchen Garden in Delhi for over four decades.  Learn the basics principles of gardening and how to make the most of what you have. They share their experiences of creating a sustainable garden, how they leaned from their mistakes, what you need to start, what you must watch out for, to grow a garden in tune with Nature and her seasons. Even with limited "free" time you can have a productive garden that brings joy.The Gardening Session will be followed by Tea in the garden. Jayasri and Anita have a delicious, colourful Spring-themed menu planned for you.

location.address: Sector 30, Noida, Uttar Pradesh 201303, India

name: Sip & Paint Workshop
description: Welcome to our Sip & Paint Ceramic Workshop!Join us for a slow, hands-on session at our studio; an afternoon of clay, colour, and a drink on us.In this workshop, you’ll get to paint a handcrafted ceramic piece. You can choose a handcrafted piece from our curated selection at the studio (mugs, sippers, travel cups, matcha bowls, dish plates, and more).We provide food-safe glazes, and once your piece is painted, we’ll glaze and fire it for you, making it ready for everyday use (microwave and dishwasher safe).You can choose to pick up your finished piece from the studio or have it couriered to you (charges applicable as per actuals).We can’t wait to host you 🫶

location.address: Gurugram, Haryana, India

name: South American Supper Club On a Farm
description: Welcome to V’s Supper Stories, Supper Club — where food is slow, stories are shared, and the table is the experience.Every edition of V’s Supper Stories is rooted in a different mood — a story told through ingredients, plating, and pacing. The current theme?Tucked away from the chaos of the city, this Latin American supper club at The Farm Collective, Gurgaon invites you to slow down and savour an evening that feels both grounding and indulgent.Set against a serene farm backdrop, this sundowner experience (5:00 PM – 7:30 PM) is designed as a gentle escape from the noise and rush of everyday life. As the sun dips, you’ll be welcomed into a thoughtfully curated dining experience that brings together bold Latin American flavours with the simplicity of fresh, seasonal produce.Expect dishes crafted with farm-to-table ingredients, where vegetables are harvested fresh and transformed into vibrant plates that celebrate both flavour and origin. The menu reflects a balance of rustic authenticity and creative expression — rooted in Latin American traditions, yet shaped by the rhythm of the land around you.More than just a meal, this is an invitation to pause, connect, and experience food in its most honest form — shared over a table that values community, conversation, and the joy of eating well.Come for the sunset, stay for the feeling.Follow along at @vsupperstories for glimpses of the table, the food, and the people who bring it to life.

location.address: 83J7+2F, Ghamroj, Haryana 122102, India

name: FLAABH Fit: Tropical Grind
description: Step into a tropical wellness party like no other as we celebrate GetWetFit’s anniversary with FLAABH Fit: Tropical Grind. Set against vibrant beachy vibes, this immersive experience begins with our signature Fitness on Water session, followed by an energizing Spin Cycle routine and a refreshing Ice Plunge recovery to recharge your body.Beyond the workouts, unwind and soak in the celebration with curated mocktails, delicious food & beverages, skincare experiences, exclusive goodie bags, and plenty of tropical surprises throughout the evening.Think sunshine energy, beach club vibes, music, movement, and a community that celebrates wellness together.Dress Code: Beachy 🌴

name: Salad Making Workshop
description: Join us for a hands-on Salad Making Workshop where you’ll learn how to create vibrant, flavour-packed salads using fresh farm-to-table and hydroponic greens from Agama.In this interactive session, you'll guide you through the basics of building the perfect salad — balancing greens, grains, proteins, toppings and house-made dressings to create bowls that are nutritious, satisfying and delicious.Whether you’re looking to eat healthier, explore plant-based cooking, or simply learn new recipes, this workshop will give you practical tips you can easily recreate at home.

location.address: M-18, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Greater Kailash, New Delhi, Delhi 110048, India

name: #lecture23: The Trees of Delhi
description: #lecture23: Trees of Delhi: From Empire and Ecology to the Politics of Trees in DelhiA river tamarind tree grows where it shouldn’t — on the edge of Bhalswa landfill.You would imagine why and how it get there?This question opens up a much larger story about trees in the city. Far from being static parts of the landscape, trees have travelled across continents through ships, trade routes, botanical experiments, and colonial ambitions. Delhi’s urban forests carry traces of these journeys.This lecture explores how trees became entangled with empire, science, and city-making. From Mughal gardens and British forestry to post-independence plantation drives and global climate targets, the trees around us reflect shifting ideas about nature, governance, and belonging.Join us for an afternoon of stories, ideas, and conversation about the hidden lives of trees in the city.About the SpeakerNirjesh Gautam is currently researching at the Centre for Urban Ecology & Sustainability. He is an independent researcher and writer interested in human–nature interactions in urban areas. His work focuses on neglected green spaces, urban biodiversity, and the politics shaping city environments. Through education, storytelling, and art, he explores how nature persists within cities and convenes a collective documenting urban ecology across Delhi-NCR.📌 Note: Alcohol will be served only to guests aged 25 and above. Non-alcoholic options will be available. Attendance implies consent to being filmed for event documentation.

location.address: D Block, D12, Market, above Bank Of India, Vasant Vihar, New Delhi, Delhi 110057, India

name: CUTTACK SPECIAL EVENING AT GRUHA
description: Step into an evening of nostalgia and flavour as Gruha brings alive the vibrant street food culture of Cuttack. From the iconic comfort of dahi bara aloo dum to a thoughtfully curated chaat experience inspired by bustling old-city lanes, the supper table celebrates the soul of Odisha’s food traditions. The highlight of the evening features the beloved pairing of soft chakuli and slow-cooked mutton curry, a meal deeply rooted in home kitchens, festive gatherings and Sunday indulgence.This supper experience is designed as a warm coming together of community, memory and regional taste — where every dish tells a story of streets, celebrations and shared heritage.

location.address: C-40/C-1, Block C, Ardee City, Sector 52, Gurugram, Haryana 122003, India

name: A Modern Bengali Dinner Party
description: Step into an evening where food, stories, and strangers come together around one table. Toontooni’s Table, the modern Bengali supper club invites you to an intimate dining experience in Gurgaon designed for people who believe the best conversations happen over good food. At this edition of the supper club, guests will gather for a thoughtfully curated Bengali meal that celebrates tradition while reimagining it for a contemporary table. Expect a multi-course menu that moves through the flavours of Bengal—mustard, posto, seasonal vegetables, and slow-cooked meats—presented with a modern twist that reflects the spirit of Toontooni’s Table. Seats are intentionally limited to keep the experience personal and conversational. Whether you come solo or with a friend, expect an evening of slow dining, warm hospitality, and the joy of discovering Bengali cuisine in a setting that feels like home.Come hungry, curious, and ready to meet your table.

location.address: Baharampur Naya, Sector 62, Gurugram, Haryana 122098, India

name: Handmade Maps: Craft Delhi's Hidden History
description: Unleash your inner cartographer! In this 2-hour hands-on workshop, create personalized handmade maps of Delhi's heritage sites using pens, inks, and collage. Led by architect-urban designer. Perfect for explorers, artists, and history buffs.

location.address: Saket, New Delhi, Delhi, India

name: ALT EFF Film Club - Gurugram | 17th April
description: If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 10 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.With snacks. Always snacks.APRIL CURATION: "The Climate Baby Dilemma"Synopsis: "The Climate Baby Dilemma" by filmmaker Victoria Lean follows science writer Britt Wray as she explores the decision to have children amid climate change. Over three years, she engages with activists and parents, reflecting generational concerns. Featuring Sarain Fox and Severn Suzuki, the film offers insights on transforming climate anxiety into action.PROGRAMME17th April 2026, Friday7:00 - 9:00 PM: The Climate Baby Dilemma | Victoria Lean | Canada | 44 mins | English | 2022All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Film screeningGroup discussionLight refreshmentsCommunity spaceStudent Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening). CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFAll Living Things Environmental Film Festival (ALT EFF) is the world’s largest decentralised film festival and a premiere showcase for environmental cinema.Across its annual festival, monthly Film Club, schools initiatives, film fund, and year round curations, ALT EFF nurtures stories grounded in care, collective responsibility, stewardship, and an abiding love for the natural world.Spanning countries, genres, languages, and forms, it brings together stories reimagining our relationship with all living things.

name: Delhi Coffee Crawl at altogether experimental
description: An sunset coffee experience at ATE Glasshouse, designed as a part of the Delhi Coffee Crawl.This stop is built around a guided coffee session where you’ll move through three different styles of coffee, each served one after the other. Along the way, we’ll also share the story behind the coffees, the space, and what makes this experience unique.Set during the golden hour, the space opens up into a calm, sunlit setting that transitions beautifully as the light changes. It’s designed to be slow, interactive, and easy to engage with whether you’re coming alone or with someone.Your experience includes a curated coffee sequence along with a dessert pairing.We’ve put this together to help you experience coffee in a more thoughtful and engaging way, beyond just ordering a cup.ATE Glasshouse | 22nd March | 5:30pm onwards

name: Wine Tasting: Sparkling & Rosé Sundowner
description: Delhi wine lovers, our next community special wine tasting will have your Sunday evening plans sorted! ☀️🥂Join Sommelier Devati Mallick (Founder & Curator, Drink With D Wine Experiences) for a Sparkling & Rosé Sundowner at Miss Pinto: a super relaxed, wine-filled hangout at one of our favourite neighbourhood spots for wine and a good time!This one’s a little different from our usual sit-down tastings (more FUN!). Think a breezy walkaround wine discovery sesh while the sun sets, with a soundtrack groovy enough to keep the mood light and happy. We’ll set up a wine bar with 10 wines pouring through the evening that are going to be our absolute TOP summer picks this season: lively sparklings, pretty rosés and bright, refreshing whites you're going to fall in love with.Miss Pinto will be putting together one epic grazing table plus hot European-inspired pass-around bites from their new menu, so the plan is simple: sip, snack, and hang out with our community wine lovers! Minimal wine geekery, maximum good times.At its heart, this is still very much a Drink With D community evening: a room full of curious wine lovers, easy conversations between glasses, and a few fun wine games to keep things lively between sips, just the way we love it!Come with friends, come solo, come ready to make a few new wine friends along the way. Don't miss our summer special wine tasting!30 seats only. Come swirl, sip and ease into sunset with us! 🍷✨

name: PAWS & PLAY AT AGAMA
description: Paws & Play at Agama is a relaxed social gathering designed for pet parents and their pets to spend a fun, easygoing afternoon together.Bring your furry companions along to meet other pets, enjoy a welcoming pet-friendly space, and take part in a few games planned for both pets and their humans.While the pets socialize, pet parents can enjoy Agama’s signature plant-based farm-to-table food and drinks.We are a plant based, farm to table cafe known for our fresh ingredient-driven menu, featuring produce grown at our own farm along with mushrooms cultivated in-house and hydroponically grown greens. Our kitchen focuses on thoughtful plant-based dishes that are comforting, flavourful, and consciously sourced.

name: Food as Medicine
description: Join us for a 1.5 hour interactive workshop on the farm with Jaskrit, founder of Inner Garden and Atithi. Jaskrit is a farm-to-table cook, herbalist, and ethnobotanist with a passion for healing our environments and bodies through connecting with nature. In this 2-part workshop, we will learn how to sprout our own nutrient-dense microgreens and make our own herbal teas using fresh, seasonal herbs.

location.address: C49J+RH8, Baliawas, Haryana 122101, India

name: What Gets Funded Gets Published
description: Join us for a lecture in a bar, that asks a question most news consumers rarely pause to consider:Who really pays for the news you read?About the LectureFor most audiences today, journalism functions much like any other product or service, shaped by business models, incentives, and the financial structures that sustain it. Just like education, healthcare, or streaming platforms, news organisations must operate within economic systems that determine what survives and what disappears.So what happens when journalism is funded by corporate advertising? Or when governments become the primary source of financial support? But, there's a way for audiences to intersect - healthily, happily, with utility - with news organisations that aren't run by corporate or governments. This talk will explain how.About the SpeakerAbhinandan Sekhri is the cofounder and CEO of Newslaundry, one of India’s leading independent digital news platforms built on a subscription-driven model.He also cofounded Small Screen, a production house creating television, film, and digital content. Before launching Newslaundry, he worked as a producer, director, and writer on several television projects, including the award-winning food and travel show Highway on My Plate, as well as satirical news programmes such as Gustaakhi Maaf and The Great Indian Tamasha.Earlier in his career, he worked as a reporter with the India Today Group from 1995 to 1999.Through Newslaundry, he has been a vocal advocate for reader-funded journalism and media accountability in India.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Sanctuary in an Age of Suspicion & Surveillance
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a question both urgent and uncomfortable:Who decides what a refugee means?About the LectureDisplacement is not new. People have fled war, persecution, famine, and disaster for centuries. Yet today, the word refugee evokes anxiety, suspicion, and hostility across much of the world.In South Asia, a region shaped by partitions, migrations, and ongoing displacement, tensions between state sovereignty and refugee rights are intensifying. Anti-refugee sentiment feeds policy shifts, and policy shifts in turn reshape public perception. Meanwhile, the international refugee protection regime, once seen as a moral and legal achievement, is being steadily eroded, often by the very states that helped build it.What then is the future of refugee protection?This lecture traces the evolution of international refugee law, its conventions, norms, institutions, and political foundations, before turning to the contemporary landscape of displacement. It examines how states are responding to increasingly complex crises, and how narratives around refugees are being constructed, circulated, and weaponised.About the SpeakerDivita Shandilya is a Protection Associate at United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) in the Greater Delhi Area. Her work spans program management, protection research, and policy engagement in contexts of displacement and international development.Prior to her current role, she worked with ActionAid India, where she led policy and research initiatives focused on South-South cooperation and global governance. Her research interests include gender, refugee protection, international development architecture, and mechanisms of global cooperation. She has been closely involved in multi-country research on refugee women and their experiences of displacement and exclusion, and has contributed to international advocacy platforms including Civil20, BRICS Feminist Watch, and Civil BRICS.Her work sits at the intersection of policy, research, and lived experience — engaging both institutional frameworks and the human stories they seek to govern.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Basement, Ground floor and First floor Building no. 12, Panchsheel Community Centre, Shahpur Jat, Panchsheel Park, New Delhi, Delhi 110017, India

name: Who Gets To Belong In A City?
description: Who is a city actually built for?About the LecturePublic space is often described as neutral, open, accessible, equally available to all.But everyday experience tells a different story.Who sits comfortably in a park and who scans their surroundings. Who lingers and who rushes. Who takes up space and who shrinks. Who feels at home and who feels watched. These patterns are not incidental. They reflect deeper structures of power embedded in design, governance, and investment.This lecture examines how cities are shaped not just by infrastructure, but by assumptions about whose presence is anticipated, and whose is treated as exceptional.Drawing from fieldwork across Indian cities, including on-ground urban transformation projects in Ayodhya and research in Delhi, alongside global scholarship on gender-responsive planning, The aim of this lecture is not only to critique existing models,  but to offer new ways of seeing, imagining, and building cities.About the SpeakerRuchita Bansal is an urban practitioner and researcher working at the intersection of public space, mobility, and gender in Indian cities. Her work explores how infrastructure decisions shape everyday urban experience, particularly for women and marginalised communities.She has been closely involved in large-scale urban transformation initiatives, including façade improvement and public realm interventions in Ayodhya, working directly with planners, contractors, and local authorities to translate design vision into execution under complex, time-bound conditions.Ruchita is also the founder of SheCity India, a platform documenting and analysing how women experience Indian cities. Through research, storytelling, public dialogue, and the Human City Podcast, she challenges the assumption that public space is neutral — arguing instead that cities mirror the values and power structures embedded within their systems.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Basement, Ground floor and First floor Building no. 12, Panchsheel Community Centre, Shahpur Jat, Panchsheel Park, New Delhi, Delhi 110017, India

name: Safdarjung Tomb Heritage Walk
description: Safdarjung’s Tomb Heritage WalkJoin us for a journey into the fading glow of Mughal splendour at Safdarjung's Tomb — often called the *last flicker of Mughal grandeur* in Delhi.Built in 1754 for Safdarjung, the powerful Nawab of Awadh and Prime Minister of the Mughal court, this monument stands at the twilight of an empire — grand in ambition, yet carrying within it the quiet signs of decline.Its story is as fascinating as its form. From courtly intrigues and shifting loyalties to the architectural choices that reveal a changing political landscape, Safdarjung’s life opens a compelling window into 18th-century Delhi.We will also trace a lesser-known layer of its history — how this tomb was constructed by reusing material from a pre-existing Mughal structure, quite literally building one memory over another.To truly understand Delhi — its rise, its resilience, and its gradual transitions — a visit to Safdarjung’s Tomb is essential.Come, walk with us through the final chapter of Mughal architectural brilliance — where grandeur meets gravity, and history lingers in every arch.

location.address: Airforce Golf Course, Delhi Race Club, New Delhi, Delhi 110003, India

name: Tracing Djnns of Kotla Ferozshah
description: TRACING THE DJINNS of FEROZSHAH KOTLA FORTAs twilight deepens and shadows lengthen across the old walls, we will gather at the main gate of Feroz Shah Kotla. Before us lie crumbling stone portals, hollowed arches, and silent courtyards that once bustled with life in the 14th century under Sultan Feroz Shah Tughlaq.This is no ordinary heritage walk. That evening, we will tread the boundary between history and myth — stepping into the realm of the djinns said to haunt these ruins, to listen, to judge, and sometimes to grant wishes.Led by expert storyteller and myth-hunter, the walk will weave together folklore, historical lore, and spooky tales when moonlight meets masonry. Expect atmospheric narration, dramatic pauses, unexpected twists — and maybe a few ghostly encounters.Not for the faint hearted !!Highlights * A guided evening walk through ruins, hidden chambers, and alleyways* Folklore and legends of djinns haunting the fort* Dramatic storytelling under the evening skies* Occasional “mystery stops” and whispers in the dark* A themed ritual (writing wishes / invoking legends) in the end.

location.address: J6MV+X87, Balmiki Basti, Vikram Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi, 110002, India

name: Nizamuddin Heritage walk on Sufism & Poetry
description: In the Sufi Bylanes of Nizamuddin — A Heritage Walk of Stories, Shrines & SilenceStep into the tender, time-worn lanes of Nizamuddin, where the fragrance of incense mingles with qawwali echoes and centuries of faith, love, and longing still breathe through stone and sky.Join us for a soulful heritage walk through the sacred precincts of Nizamuddin Dargah, as we trace the footsteps of saints, poets, patrons, and wanderers who made this neighbourhood a living archive of Delhi’s Sufi soul.On this journey, we will also pause at:✨ Ghalib’s Mazar — remembering the poet of longing and lament✨ Chausath Khamba — a marble pavilion of quiet grace and symmetry✨ Atagha Khan’s Tomb — a Mughal-era resting place of power and piety✨ Pateh Shah’s Dargah — where devotion meets deep serenity. Along the way, we’ll listen to stories of shrines and seekers, of migration and memory, of music, miracles, and mysticism — stories that still whisper through these alleys at dusk.

location.address: Bharat Scouts and Guides Marg, opposite Humayun Tomb Park, Nizamuddin, Hazrat Nizamuddin, National Zoological Park, Sundar Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi 110013, India

description: This isn’t your usual wine tasting.And it’s not meant to be.This is a guided experience  where you understandwhy brie works with one wine,why goat cheese behaves differently,why chocolate surprises you with another,and why some wines simply need time.You’re guided step by step by a sommelier who lives and breathes wine.You taste. You ask. You talk. You linger.You’ll share the table with people you haven’t met before, and one of kind experienceThis will include 5 tasting wines paired thoughtfully with small bites. Additional wines can be purchased at the event later.This event will take place at our residence in Gurgaon. name: Pakhala Pop Up : A Summer Table from Odisha
description: Pakhala Pop-Up: A Summer Table from OdishaA Rosei Ghara x Kartik’s Kitchen CollaborationAs the Delhi sun begins to turn warmer, we bring to the table a ritual from coastal Odisha — a meal designed for summer.Pakhala is not just fermented rice. It is comfort, cooling, seasonal wisdom, and memory served in a bowl.On 21st & 22nd March, from 12 pm onwards, Rosei Ghara, in collaboration with Kartik’s Kitchen, invites you to experience an Odia summer table — thoughtfully curated, slow, and rooted in tradition.What to Expect:At the centre sits Pakhala — lightly fermented rice, cooling and probiotic-rich — accompanied by an abundant spread of classic summer sides. Expect crisp fries, smoky mash, leafy greens, lentil badis, seasonal vegetables, and carefully spiced accompaniments — each dish balancing texture, heat, tang, and crunch.Why It’s Special• A rare opportunity to experience Odisha’s iconic summer meal in Delhi• A collaboration between two kitchens that celebrate regional Indian flavours• Limited seating to preserve the intimacy of the table• Hosted in a private residence in Jangpura Extension (complete address shared post-booking)• A slow, immersive lunch designed around seasonality and traditionPakhala is typically eaten for lunch in Odia homes — and we invite you to experience it exactly as it is meant to be enjoyed: unhurried, generous, and shared.📍 Venue: Jangpura Extension, Delhi🗓️ Dates: 21st & 22nd March🕛 Time: 12 pm onwards✨ Limited seats | Community dining experienceCome curious. Come hungry.Leave with a new understanding of how Odisha does summer.

location.address: Jungpura Extension, Jangpura, New Delhi, Delhi, India

name: HE SAID SHE SAID: Comedy Drama
description: DREAMCATCHERS THEATRE CLUB, GURGAONPRESENTSHE SAID SHE SAID A comedy of mannersFilled with laughter and drama with sharp wit as this classic comedy drama take you on a journey through the quirks of relationships, perceptions, and the misunderstandings that arise from everyday conversations.  Synopsis : He Said She Said by Alice Gerstenberg.  He Said She Said, offers a captivating story centered around a carefully orchestrated conflict by a socialite. The audience is left questioning who is truly telling the truth as two conflicting perspectives unfold, showcasing the chaos and confusion that can result from careless words.Join us for an engaging and highly entertaining live theatre experience that weaves together clever dialogues, unexpected twists, and comical moments that will have you reflecting on the ups and downs of human interactions.Cast: Madhu Kandhari, Sangeeta Dixit Chakraborty, Sangeeta Rathi, Nikhil RajeAbout the groupDreamcatchers, the vibrant theater group based in Gurgaon, is a creative powerhouse bringing fresh energy to the stage with our innovative storytelling! Our passionate team consists of enthusiastic theater lovers—actors, singers, musicians, dancers, writers, and artists—dedicated to nurturing the performing arts in our country through their talents and support. Don’t miss out on these incredible stories—stay tuned for a memorable theatrical experience!

name: 4 sessions package TAI CHI WITH RASHID
description: VALID FOR 4 SESSIONS UNTIL END OF MAY 2026 Join the Tai Chi Practice Circle with sensei Rashid Ansari for cultivating wellness, calmness, grounding and inner balance with the transformative practices of Tai Chi and Qi Gong. Held on Sundays at Lilanoor Centre. Hosted by IT'S ALL GOOD.Starting March 15th.Cost : Rs. 4800/- for 4 sessions (Student Rs. 2000/-). IMPORTANT NOTE: To book this 4-SESSION PAGKAGE, please click on the session given: MAY 31st. It means that you may attend ANY 4 Sunday Tai Chi sessions until the end of May, 2026. Thank you. [You may also book a single session as a DROP-IN (Rs. 1500/- per session; Student Rs. 600/-). See specific date in the main listing for booking, or simply drop in and pay on the spot.] ***Both Tai Chi and Qi Gong offer a unique perspective of being a Participant and Observer simultaneously.Tai chi, often called Meditation in Motion, is an incredibly potent system of mind body synergy and a block and tackle system, working on the joints and angles of the body.regulates blood pressure and hypertension.the different movements work on the different organs and glands, helping and energising them to work at optimum potency.strengthens the muscles and tendons, aligns the skeletal and neuromuscular system.helps to deal with anxiety and mental health issues.can be practiced by anybody - young and old alike.Rashid Ansari has been practicing/teaching the martial arts , dance, movement, choreography and holistic health/wellness for over 50 years now, both in India and various parts of the world. In addition to martial arts, Ansari is a contemporary dance instructor and choreographer. He has explored physical theatre and theatre movement, integrating these disciplines into his teaching methodology. He founded and serves as the artistic director of Mobius Strip, a dance theatre company, and Earth Rhythms, a world music ensemble. He also conducts classes and workshops in “Dance your Senses - The 5 Rhythms.” A organic movement methodology based on the individual’s own intrinsic sense of movement and emotions - as a means of finding one’s own movement and dealing with physical, emotional and intellectual blocks.***IT'S ALL GOOD | The Science and Art of JoyMany people feel stuck, exasperated, low, or just plain bored in their lives. We provide a platform for fun and engaging experiences that foster insight and empowerment.Connect | Learn | Thrive!INSTAGRAM LINK CLICK HERE

name: Curated Tour of Lodhi Art District by St+art India
description: Lodhi Art DistrictLodhi Art District is India’s first ongoing art district. An open-air museum by St+art India Foundation, the pedestrian-friendly colony houses over 65+ murals by renowned local and international artists. The area serves as evidence of the possibility of reimagining public spaces, bringing us back in touch with our city, its history, and the many narratives that illuminate it. Sign up for an immersive walk guided by a St+art team member to discover the murals, consider how public spaces can be utilised, and become a part of this cultural transformation.Meeting Point: Indian Habitat Centre, Gate 3About the TourThe curated tours are a walkthrough of the vibrant public art district. They offer an interactive exploration through which St+art introduces new ways of experiencing contemporary art and the cityscape. These tours provide a complete experience, delving into the historical aspects of the location, the stories behind the project, and the murals themselves. Participants will actively witness the enhancement of the city and the reimagining of its public spaces. A tour leader from the St+art team will provide insights into the background and context of the artworks, the area, and its cultural significance in the city. (Nearby attractions include Safdarjung Tomb, Lodhi Garden, and Khan Market). About St+artSt+art India Foundation contributes to urban regeneration and community living through contemporary urban art projects. The foundation enables a vision for democratised public spaces through interdisciplinary art interventions that are rooted in the social context.Since 2014, with the support of Asian paints, the foundation has organised multiple festivals and public art projects in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Chandigarh and Coimbatore among others. Each public art district in the country and editions of festivals bring curated interventions to civic spaces that are embedded in urban culture, and use art as a tool to reimagine how public spaces can be utilised. For the foundation, which has been responsible for making urban art a movement in India, public art interventions are a celebration of the street as a canvas for visual creativity.

location.address: India Habitat Centre Gate 3, Vardhman Marg, Block 20, Institutional Area, Lodi Colony, New Delhi, Delhi 110003, India

name: Dinner with strangers by StepOut (South Delhi)
description: Serendipity on a TableStepOut Dinners bring strangers together at some of the city’s best restaurants for an evening that feels easy, warm, and unexpectedly meaningful.You are seated at a small, thoughtfully curated table with people you would likely never meet otherwise. Different backgrounds, different stories, one shared meal. The crowd is balanced, open minded, and there by choice, not chance. No loud networking. No awkward icebreakers. Just good food, unforced conversations, and the kind of listening that rarely happens in daily life.The restaurants are carefully chosen. Comfortable, well loved spaces where you can settle in and talk without rushing. The focus is not on the menu alone, but on the atmosphere that allows connection to unfold naturally.Some nights are light and playful. Some go deeper than expected. Some surprise you quietly. That is the point.StepOut Dinners are not about dating or outcomes. They are about discovery. Showing up. Sitting down. Letting the table do its quiet work.Sometimes, all it takes is one seat at the right table for life to feel a little more open. For more information, visit www.stepout.world

location.address: South Delhi, New Delhi, Delhi, India
